pages.full_text:
April 9th  2005.
	 At 11 we set off for __Aston Cantlow__.
On the way we had to stop in a layby for the brakes to get sorted - for the 3rd year running they've wrong straight after the winter. After 30 minutes we drove on.
	Thomas has just come back from US & Rome. William has started a CFA course in London. Peter has bought an internet cafe in Shanghai. John has stays at home to revise for the IB. Charles has brought revision along for his GCSEs. (He'll be 16 next week)
Pope JP2 was [illegible] buried yesterday.
Prince Charles is marrying Camilla today.
	We parked in the campsite, surrounded by plastic houses, + walked along the footpath to the pub, The King's Head. One of the best pub meals ever. Charles ha[d] [continues overleaf]
pages.notes: Right edge cut off by gutter — last line reads 'Charles ha[d]' with text continuing on right page. The struck-through word before 'buried' is illegible (possibly 'for' or 'just'). 'stays' appears to be a slip for 'stays' (diarist wrote 'has stays' — verbatim preserved). Page number not visible in top-left corner of this scan.

pages.full_text:
beef sausage. Simon & Eve had 5hour lamb + pumpkin + thyme mash wi rosemary sauce. Too full for pud. Back to the van for hot drinks.
	We napped + walked around fields outside the village. Flat, lots of apple trees, very pleasant but cold. Lambs. Tonic keeps licking his paws. We had a cup of tea + read. Supper we had in the van because the pub was already fully booked.
	The campsite has lots of ducks + geese + a third Bellamy award. They recycle rubbish. The woman who showed us to the pitch has 2 children. She's lived here since she was 8.
	The church has a lovely Millenium Sampler showing types of Embroidery stitches - Bayeux, Black Work, [illegible] Tudor Gold work, Jacobean Stump Work, Victorian flowers... Simon + Eve spent the evening with Coats + Woolley hat [?]s on. V. cold.
pages.notes: Page number 51 visible top-right. The text at the top is a continuation from the left page ('Charles ha[d] beef sausage'). 'Millenium' spelled with single 'n' — verbatim preserved. One embroidery stitch type illegible between 'Black Work,' and 'Tudor Gold work'. The last word before 'on. V. cold.' appears to be 'hat [?]s' — possibly 'hats' but partially obscured. 'wi' is presumably an abbreviation for 'with'.
